focus: Design the crash-safe incremental style indexer and sync state machine grounded in current styles/_engine manifest invalidation and system-spec-kit incremental indexing/mutation code.
noveltyJustification: 8 of 8 findings were fully new state-machine, publication, recovery, or rollback conclusions relative to iterations 1-3.
ruledOut: In-place rebuilding of the active database or vector shard
Hard deletion after one absence observation
Vector result commit without retrieval-hash/profile CAS

summary: Use DISCOVER, VERIFY, PARSE_VALIDATE, COMMIT, VECTOR_DRAIN, and PUBLISH with hash-authoritative freshness.
Atomically upsert normalized source and retrieval document rows; let FTS triggers participate in the same transaction and advance mtime only after success.
Key idempotent vector work by style, retrieval hash, and profile; CAS-check freshness before atomic success publication.
Quarantine absence, tombstone after independent confirmation, and defer hard deletion until retention and rebuild safety gates pass.
Publish immutable corpus generation and current pointer in the same transaction as style changes; readers bind one snapshot.
Full rebuild uses a verified, WAL-checkpointed staged database/shard and an atomic switch that preserves concurrent old-generation readers.
Durable transaction, queue, and staging evidence makes every crash point resumable without claiming uncommitted success.
Retain the prior generation so rollback is a guarded pointer reversal before any checkpoint-level restoration.
